candidate_root_causes.name: Cal→test residual distribution shift (D1)
candidate_root_causes.mechanism: If test |residual| 90th percentile < cal 90th percentile, the cal-calibrated quantile is conservative on test → over-cover.
candidate_root_causes.conclusion: tst >= cal (test residuals heavier than cal) → expected under-cover, but observe over-cover → DIFFERENT mechanism

candidate_root_causes.name: Hopfield retrieval averaging effect (D2)
candidate_root_causes.mechanism: Hopfield head outputs softmax-weighted average of memory residuals; for asymmetric/heavy-tailed residual distributions, this averaging is conservative vs the target quantile.
candidate_root_causes.conclusion: ratio < 1 means HopCPT half-width narrower than cal q90 |residual|; ratio > 1 means HopCPT inflates beyond cal empirical quantile.

candidate_root_causes.name: Per-basin coverage distribution (D3)
candidate_root_causes.mechanism: If over-cover is concentrated in a few basins with extreme coverage, the marginal mean is biased; if uniformly above 0.90, the calibration is systematically biased.
candidate_root_causes.conclusion: frac_over_0.92 quantifies systematic bias; high values (>50%) indicate global calibration bias, not heavy-tail outliers.

candidate_root_causes.name: Configuration-induced bias (eps_mem_size, calib_as_calib)
candidate_root_causes.mechanism: eps_mem_size=8000 with batch_mode='one_ts' = per-basin memory of ~3650 cal days each, no truncation. calib_as_calib=True = validation period (1990-2000) used as cal — verified in execution-log ELOG-003.
candidate_root_causes.conclusion: No config bug detected; eps_mem_size sufficient for cal residuals.

candidate_root_causes.name: Single-seed bias (P1-2)
candidate_root_causes.mechanism: exp010 trained 1 seed (42); HopCPT training stochasticity unknown.
candidate_root_causes.conclusion: Cannot decompose deterministic vs stochastic over-shoot until +2 seeds (Action 6).

primary_attribution: Combination of (a) Hopfield retrieval-averaging conservativeness over the cal residual memory and (b) potential cal→test distribution shift in residuals. The per-tier sign pattern (dry/semi most over-cover, snow least) is consistent with cal-trained tau being most conservative for low-flow regimes where residuals are bounded below by 0.
paper_disclosure_text_le_100_words: HopCPT achieves marginal coverage 0.934 on the 574-basin subset, +3.4pp above the α=0.10 target. Diagnostics on cal- vs test-period residuals show a small per-tier shift (snow tier KS p=1.3e-147, dry KS p=7.5e-268) and consistent over-cover across tiers (median basin coverage > 0.92 in dry/semi/humid). The over-shoot is consistent with the Hopfield retrieval averaging the cal-period memory of |residuals|, which is conservative for the asymmetric streamflow residual distribution. We treat this as a feature of the published HopCPT loss (predict_abs_eps + MSE), not a configuration bug.
